name: Creole Gumbo
recipeIngredient: Salt, freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 lbs Fresh Okra, cut into 1/2-inch lengths
1 Large Ham Bone, halved or quartered
1 lb Boneless Veal Stew Meat, cut into 1-inch cubes
2 lbs Fresh Raw Shrimp, shelled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the bacon pieces in half and put them in a large soup kettle. Place over low heat and cook until the bacon gives up its fat. Remove from heat, drain on paper towels, and set aside.
2. Add the onions to the bacon drippings in the kettle. Cook over moderate heat until golden brown, stirring constantly.
3. Add garlic, bay leaves, green pepper, thyme (fresh or dried), sugar, salt, and pepper. Cook slowly until the green pepper is wilted.
4. Add cut okra and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Heat oil in a large skillet. Add ham bone, chicken wings, and veal cubes. Cook over medium heat, stirring to brown meats on all sides.
6. Pour off any excess fat from the skillet. Stir the contents with a wooden spoon to loosen brown particles clinging to the bottom and sides of the pan. Mix this into the kettle.
7. Add parsley, tomatoes, Tabasco sauce, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, shrimp, crab meat, oysters (with their liquid), and reserved bacon to the kettle. Pour in enough water to cover all ingredients, bring the gumbo to a boil, then turn off the heat.
8. Stir in the file powder and mix well. Serve with a mound of cooked rice in the center of each soup plate, ladling the gumbo around the rice.
